porous.title: Porous Boundaries
porous.tagline: you let everyone in — and it costs you
porous.desc: You absorb other people's emotions, take on their problems, and struggle to tell where you end and someone else begins. Saying no feels like a betrayal. You over-share, over-give, and then wonder why you feel hollow.
porous.advice: A boundary isn't a wall — it's a filter. Start with one small "no" this week. The discomfort fades but the self-respect doesn't.
youProbably: Feel responsible for other people's emotions
Attract people who take more than they give

passive.title: Passive Boundaries
passive.tagline: you know what you need — you just can't say it
passive.desc: You have the words. You even rehearse them in the shower. But in the moment, they dissolve. You hint, you hope, you swallow. The boundary exists inside you — it just hasn't made it to your mouth yet.
passive.advice: Start with low-stakes situations. Text the boundary before saying it face-to-face. Every rep makes the next one easier. Your voice deserves volume.
youProbably: Say "it's fine" when it absolutely is not
Build resentment silently until you explode
Feel surprised when people cross lines you never voiced

healthy.title: Healthy Boundaries
healthy.tagline: you can love and still say no
healthy.desc: You've learned — maybe the hard way — that protecting your energy isn't selfish. You say no without cruelty, yes without resentment, and hold space without losing yourself.
healthy.advice: Boundaries are muscles, not achievements. They need maintenance. Watch for situations where old patterns creep back — usually with the people who matter most.
youProbably: Disagree without it becoming a crisis
Know when to engage and when to walk away
Attract people who respect your space because you model it

rigid.title: Rigid Boundaries
rigid.tagline: the walls work — but they're lonely
rigid.desc: You built your defenses for a reason, and they work. Almost too well. People can't hurt you, but they also can't reach you. Intimacy feels like a security risk, and vulnerability is a door you sealed shut.
rigid.advice: The walls kept you safe once. Ask yourself if they still need to be this high, or if they're protecting you from a threat that's no longer there.
youProbably: Cut people off cleanly and quickly
Prefer independence over connection when stressed
Feel proud of needing no one — and sometimes empty because of it
